title: Chrysolaena cognata
taxonomy: Chrysolaena cognata is classified within the family Asteraceae. This plant is native to Brazil and is a species of flowering plant. Chrysolaena cognata is a member of the tribe Mutisieae, a group of flowering plants that are commonly found in the Americas. The classification of Chrysolaena cognata reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Asteraceae family. Further research may be necessary to confirm its exact classification.
uses: Chrysolaena cognata, a plant species native to Brazil, has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. Research has shown that extracts from the plant may have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which could contribute to its use in treating various health conditions. The plant's leaves and stems contain compounds with antimicrobial properties, which could aid in the treatment of infections. Furthermore, Chrysolaena cognata has been investigated for its potential role in protecting against oxidative stress and cell damage. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal applications and potential benefits to human health.

preparations: Chrysolaena cognata is a plant species that has been used in traditional herbal medicine, particularly in the subtropical regions of South America.
